title: Removed Static Tag Data Fallbacks
summary: Removed static analytics tag JSON fallbacks from migrated local-only Studio tag views.
effect: Migrated analytics tag views now require local runtime analytics read endpoints for tag groups, registry, aliases, and assignments. Browser-facing Studio config no longer advertises static tag-data paths, the dev Studio Jekyll overlay excludes those source JSON files, and the local route smoke fails if any migrated tag page requests static assets/studio/data/tag_*.json data.
verification: node --check passed for assets/studio/js/studio-config.js, assets/studio/js/studio-data.js, and assets/studio/js/tag-groups.js
tests/smoke/local_studio_app_tag_routes.py passed with loopback server permissions and checks for no static tag-data requests
Verified the dev Studio Jekyll build excludes tag_registry.json, tag_aliases.json, tag_assignments.json, and tag_groups.json while still publishing studio_config.json
